The final matrix must be much more detailed.\r\n\r\n## Category A: Animation does not run at all\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* scene is registered but never called,\r\n* component exists but is never invisible\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* GSAP animates an empty target array,\r\n* the target is in a hidden source tree,\r\n* the animation runs in an unmounted section,\r\n* the target is behind another layer,\r\n* opacity or display prevents visibility,\r\n* a cloned PageFlip source receives the animation instead of the visible page,\r\n* broad selectors animate offscreen elements,\r\n* the scene completes semantically while no visible object moved.\r\n\r\n## Category C: Animation runs poorly\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* abrupt,\r\n* too fast,\r\n* too slow,\r\n* visually weak,\r\n* generic fade,\r\n* disconnected from the physical object,\r\n* poor easing,\r\n* no anticipation,\r\n* no settle,\r\n* no secondary motion,\r\n* timing does not match sound,\r\n* animation begins before the user can see it,\r\n* animation restarts unexpectedly,\r\n* animation ends with a snap,\r\n* transition does not match the visual design,\r\n* animation looks different depending on current section.\r\n\r\n## Category D: Animation only runs once\r\n\r\nDetermine whether each one-shot is:\r\n\r\n* intentionally ceremonial,\r\n* unintentionally blocked,\r\n* session-only,\r\n* tab-only,\r\n* device-only,\r\n* account-only,\r\n* campaign-only,\r\n* event-only,\r\n* replayable,\r\n* not replayable,\r\n* replayable only before refresh,\r\n* incorrectly replayed automatically.\r\n\r\nInspect:\r\n\r\n* `sessionStorage`,\r\n* `localStorage`,\r\n* component-local refs,\r\n* event acknowledgement,\r\n* sequence tracking,\r\n* route remount behavior,\r\n* React key behavior,\r\n* CSS animation iteration counts,\r\n* viewport `once` options,\r\n* persisted â€œseenâ€ flags.\r\n\r\n## Category E: Animation does not replay correctly\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* replay button disappears after refresh,\r\n* replay works only from the Journal,\r\n* replay mutates server state,\r\n* replay uses stale event data,\r\n* replay does not restore previous section,\r\n* replay leaves elements in a transformed state,\r\n* replay works once but fails a second time,\r\n* replay conflicts with a live event.\r\n\r\n## Category F: Wrong library owns the behavior\r\n\r\nFor every questionable animation, identify:\r\n\r\n* current owner,\r\n* correct owner,\r\n* reason,\r\n* migration recommendation,\r\n* wrappers or bridge required.\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* Motion and GSAP writing transform on the same node,\r\n* GSAP page-flip scenes duplicating StPageFlip,\r\n* CSS controlling narrative order,\r\n* Lottie autoplaying independently of a GSAP ceremony,\r\n* static SVG used where a stateful Rive object is promised.\r\n\r\n## Category G: Runtime ownership conflict\r\n\r\nAudit:\r\n\r\n* GSAP versus Motion,\r\n* GSAP exists but has no event,\r\n* page turn occurs but no lifecycle scene is emitted,\r\n* inspection scene exists but opening the inspection never calls it,\r\n* ship-course exists but current position instance counts,\r\n* PageFlip instance leaks,\r\n* GSAP context reversion.\r\n\r\n## Category J: Reduced-motion defect\r\n\r\nAudit:\r\n\r\n* browser `prefers-reduced-motion`,\r\n* product full/gentle/reduced setting,\r\n* MotionConfig,\r\n* CSS media queries,\r\n* GSAP duration scaling,\r\n* PageFlip fallback,\r\n* Lottie representative frames,\r\n* Rive stable poses,\r\n* sound behavior,\r\n* keyboard and focus behavior.\r\n\r\nReduced mode must preserve narrative order and readable state without unnecessary spatial travel.\r\n\r\n## Category K: Performance thrashing,\r\n* excessive SVG nodes,\r\n* broad selectors,\r\n* hidden elements being animated,\r\n* large blur/filter costs,\r\n* mobile GPU pressure,\r\n* memory after repeated replay and navigation.\r\n\r\n## Category L: Animation is missing and should be added\r\n\r\nCreate a very large list of missing animation opportunities for every screen.\r\n\r\nEach recommendation must identify:\r\n\r\n* screen,\r\n* user action or event,\r\n* proposed animation,\r\n* correct library,\r\n* trigger,\r\n* replay policy,\r\n* reduced-motion alternative,\r\n* priority,\r\n* why it improves the experience.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 5. Baseline Findings That Must Be Verified\r\n\r\nThe following issues were identified during an external source audit. Treat these as high-priority hypotheses that must be independently verified against the exact commit.\r\n\r\nDo not blindly repeat them. Confirm, refine, disprove or expand them with code and runtime evidence.\r\n\r\n## 5.1 Production Rive assets may all be fallbacks\r\n\r\nVerify whether the following production contracts have actual `.riv` paths:\r\n\r\n* invitation seal,\r\n* journal clasp,\r\n* voyage compass,\r\n* finale mechanism.\r\n\r\nDetermine whether real product screens are receiving only static SVG fallback content.\r\n\r\nDocument the exact screens affected and whether the UI misleadingly appears to use Rive when it does not.\r\n\r\n## 5.2 New platform sign-in pages may bypass cinematic authentication\r\n\r\nCompare:\r\n\r\n* modern Player sign-in,\r\n* modern Captain/Creator sign-in,\r\n* legacy Player AccessGate,\r\n* legacy Quartermaster login.\r\n\r\nDetermine which flows call AnimationDirector and which perform direct fetch-and-route behavior.\r\n\r\n## 5.3 Invitation Ceremony may currently be a static form\r\n\r\nVerify whether invitation acceptance, decline, PIN validation, invalid state, expiration and route transitions use any of:\r\n\r\n* GSAP,\r\n* Motion,\r\n* Rive,\r\n* Lottie,\r\n* CSS state sequences.\r\n\r\n## 5.4 Registered scenes may exist only in the showcase\r\n\r\nInvestigate these scenes:\r\n\r\n* `journal-open`\r\n* `manual-page-flip`\r\n* `programmatic-page-flip`\r\n* `chapter-heading`\r\n* `prose-ink`\r\n* `seal-break`\r\n* `marker-stamp`\r\n* `ship-course`\r\n* `artifact-inspection`\r\n\r\nFor each, document all real product call sites.\r\n\r\nIf there are none, mark the scene as dead, showcase-only, legacy-only or future-contract-only.\r\n\r\n## 5.5 Chapter release may be invisible outside the Journal\r\n\r\nVerify what happens when `CHAPTER_RELEASED` arrives while the Player is viewing:\r\n\r\n* Chart,\r\n* Treasures,\r\n* Quests,\r\n* Log,\r\n* Finale.\r\n\r\nThe chapter ceremony content appears to be mounted only inside JournalWorkspace.\r\n\r\nTest whether the scene:\r\n\r\n* animates nothing,\r\n* animates unrelated background targets,\r\n* remains invisible,\r\n* moves the user to Journal,\r\n* or uses a global overlay.\r\n\r\n## 5.6 Ceremony replay may fail outside Journal\r\n\r\nTest Replay Ceremony while each Player section is active.\r\n\r\nAlso test replay:\r\n\r\n* immediately,\r\n* twice in succession,\r\n* after route navigation,\r\n* after browser refresh,\r\n* after motion-mode change,\r\n* during another live event.\r\n\r\n## 5.7 Replay state may disappear after refresh\r\n\r\nVerify whether `lastRelease` or equivalent replay information is reconstructed from a safe server snapshot or exists only in component memory.\r\n\r\n## 5.8 Generic scene-part names may animate duplicates\r\n\r\nSearch for duplicate use of:\r\n\r\n* `route-path`\r\n* `map-fog`\r\n* `map-marker-new`\r\n* `artifact-reveal`\r\n* `artifact-light`\r\n* `artifact-slot-target`\r\n* `artifact-connection-path`\r\n* `quest-note-new`\r\n* `quest-stamp`\r\n* `red-thread`\r\n* `log-entry-new`\r\n* `log-symbol-new`\r\n* `finale-ring-outer`\r\n* `finale-ring-inner`\r\n* `finale-light-path`\r\n* `workspace-light`\r\n* `lantern`\r\n* `peripheral`\r\n\r\nDetermine whether a single scene animates:\r\n\r\n* a permanent screen target,\r\n* a temporary event overlay,\r\n* hidden source content,\r\n* and visible clones simultaneously.\r\n\r\n## 5.9 PageFlip source nodes may receive GSAP animation\r\n\r\nInspect the hidden React source used by `PageFlipBook` and the cloned visible StPageFlip DOM.\r\n\r\nDetermine whether animation attributes survive into both trees and whether GSAP can animate the hidden source instead of the visible page.\r\n\r\n## 5.10 Ownership may be warning-only\r\n\r\nVerify whether a rejected animation ownership claim actually prevents the animation from running.\r\n\r\nAudit whether Motion elements register ownership in the same runtime registry or merely include a data attribute.\r\n\r\n## 5.11 Confirm Motion and GSAP transform conflicts\r\n\r\nSpecifically inspect:\r\n\r\n* Voyage Chart marker buttons,\r\n* Shipâ€™s Log entries,\r\n* artifact inspection shared-layout object,\r\n* companion navigation,\r\n* Quartermaster action buttons,\r\n* any element with both Motion layout/layoutId and `data-gsap-owned`.\r\n\r\n## 5.12 PageFlip motion mode may not update correctly\r\n\r\nVerify whether changing from full to gentle mode updates `flippingTime` on an existing PageFlip instance.\r\n\r\nInspect effect dependencies and runtime recreation.\r\n\r\n## 5.13 Lottie may reload on every mode change\r\n\r\nVerify whether changing full/gentle/reduced:\r\n\r\n* destroys the runtime,\r\n* reloads JSON,\r\n* restarts ambient loops,\r\n* replays non-looping ink effects,\r\n* creates visible blank frames.\r\n\r\n## 5.14 CSS phase waiting may hang\r\n\r\nInspect `waitForJournalPhase()`.\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* abort during a phase,\r\n* infinite animation under an actor,\r\n* browser animation promise that never resolves,\r\n* missing actor,\r\n* missing transition,\r\n* reduced mode,\r\n* unmount during wait.\r\n\r\n## 5.15 Empty target arrays may silently pass\r\n\r\nDetermine which GSAP scenes can progress through all labels with no required visible targets.\r\n\r\nAdd evidence for scenes that â€œcompleteâ€ without moving anything.\r\n\r\n## 5.16 GSAP cleanup may cause final-state snapback\r\n\r\nTest access/login success and route-changing sequences.\r\n\r\nDetermine whether GSAP context reversion restores the closed seal, door or lock before navigation completes.\r\n\r\n## 5.17 Reduced-motion behavior may have multiple competing authorities\r\n\r\nInspect:\r\n\r\n* custom motion mode,\r\n* browser preference,\r\n* MotionConfig,\r\n* CSS media query,\r\n* PageFlip reduced fallback,\r\n* Lottie mode behavior,\r\n* Rive autoplay.\r\n\r\nDetermine whether any Motion component can still animate spatially under browser reduced motion because it forgot to consume the custom mode.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 6. Required Investigation Method\r\n\r\n## 6.1 Static source inventory\r\n\r\nSearch the entire source tree for:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ngsap\r\nuseGSAP\r\ndirector.play\r\nmotion.\r\n<motion\r\nAnimatePresence\r\nlayoutId\r\nlayout\r\nwhileHover\r\nwhileTap\r\npage-flip\r\nPageFlipBook\r\nRiveStatefulObject\r\nuseRive\r\nLottieEffect\r\nlottie-web\r\n@keyframes\r\nanimation:\r\ntransition:\r\ngetAnimations\r\nsessionStorage\r\nlocalStorage\r\nprefers-reduced-motion\r\nrequestAnimationFrame\r\nsetTimeout\r\nsetInterval\r\ndata-scene-part\r\ndata-gsap-owned\r\ndata-animation-owner\r\ndata-opening-actor\r\ndata-journal-phase\r\ndata-cinematic\r\n```\r\n\r\nCreate a complete inventory of:\r\n\r\n* animation files,\r\n* animation call sites,\r\n* scene definitions,\r\n* scene consumers,\r\n* animation assets,\r\n* CSS keyframes,\r\n* transition-based state machines,\r\n* replay flags,\r\n* one-shot flags,\r\n* fallback paths,\r\n* test coverage.\r\n\r\n## 6.2 Scene reachability graph\r\n\r\nFor every registered scene, produce:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nScene name\r\nDefinition file\r\nReal production call sites\r\nDevelopment-only call sites\r\nRequired targets\r\nOptional targets\r\nCurrent root scope\r\nTriggering event/action\r\nReplay path\r\nReduced behavior\r\nKnown conflicts\r\nReachability status\r\n```\r\n\r\nReachability status must be one of:\r\n\r\n* Production active\r\n* Legacy active\r\n* Development showcase only\r\n* Registered but unreachable\r\n* Partially integrated\r\n* Fallback-only\r\n* Dead or redundant\r\n* Unknown pending runtime test\r\n\r\n## 6.3 Live browser testing\r\n\r\nWhere possible, run the real application on port 3000 and test with browser automation and manual inspection.\r\n\r\nDo not rely solely on unit tests.\r\n\r\nCapture evidence at meaningful states, not arbitrary delays.\r\n\r\nTest at minimum:\r\n\r\n* 2560 Ã— 1440\r\n* 1920 Ã— 1080\r\n* 1440 Ã— 900\r\n* 430 Ã— 932\r\n* 390 Ã— 844\r\n* 844 Ã— 390\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* full motion,\r\n* gentle motion,\r\n* product reduced motion,\r\n* browser reduced motion,\r\n* sound enabled,\r\n* sound disabled,\r\n* cached assets,\r\n* uncached assets,\r\n* first visit,\r\n* return visit,\r\n* refresh,\r\n* back/forward navigation,\r\n* replay,\r\n* skip,\r\n* failed requests,\r\n* slow requests,\r\n* offline/reconnect,\r\n* route unmount during animation.\r\n\r\nUse screenshots, Playwright traces, console output and performance evidence where appropriate.\r\n\r\nStore temporary evidence under an ignored audit directory such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nartifacts/animation-audit/\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not commit large generated traces or screenshots unless explicitly required by repository documentation policy.\r\n\r\n## 6.4 Visible-target verification\r\n\r\nFor each cinematic scene, record at runtime:\r\n\r\n* required target count,\r\n* optional target count,\r\n* visible target count,\r\n* duplicate target count,\r\n* target bounding boxes,\r\n* target opacity,\r\n* target display/visibility state,\r\n* current Player section,\r\n* scene instance ID,\r\n* animation owner,\r\n* actual properties animated.\r\n\r\nA scene must not be considered visually successful merely because its timeline completed.\r\n\r\n## 6.5 Replay testing\r\n\r\nFor every replayable scene:\r\n\r\n1. Play once.\r\n2. Replay immediately.\r\n3. Replay again.\r\n4. Navigate away and replay.\r\n5. Refresh and replay.\r\n6. Switch motion mode and replay.\r\n7. Skip midway and replay.\r\n8. Interrupt with another scene.\r\n9. Verify no server mutation occurred.\r\n10. Verify cleanup returned all runtime counts to expected values.\r\n\r\n## 6.6 One-shot analysis\r\n\r\nFor every animation that does not automatically repeat, determine exactly why.\r\n\r\nDo not write vague statements such as â€œruns once.â€\r\n\r\nWrite:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nRuns once per:\r\n- component mount\r\n- browser tab\r\n- sessionStorage key\r\n- device\r\n- campaign\r\n- acknowledged event\r\n- route visit\r\n- application session\r\n```\r\n\r\nAlso document whether that policy is correct.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 7. Required Per-Animation Matrix\r\n\r\nCreate a master matrix with one row per animation, effect, transition or missing opportunity.\r\n\r\nThe matrix must include at least these fields:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nID\r\nScreen\r\nRole\r\nComponent\r\nAnimation or proposed animation\r\nCurrent library\r\nCorrect library\r\nCurrent trigger\r\nExpected trigger\r\nCurrent replay policy\r\nRecommended replay policy\r\nRuns at all?\r\nVisible when run?\r\nRuns only once?\r\nWorks after refresh?\r\nWorks from every relevant section?\r\nFull mode result\r\nGentle mode result\r\nReduced mode result\r\nMobile result\r\nDesktop result\r\nRequired targets\r\nMissing targets\r\nDuplicate targets\r\nOwnership conflicts\r\nCleanup status\r\nFallback status\r\nPerformance concerns\r\nAccessibility concerns\r\nCurrent quality\r\nFailure severity\r\nRecommended change\r\nImplementation complexity\r\nImplementation priority\r\nTest coverage\r\nEvidence\r\nSource files\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe final matrix should be large. Do not impose an artificial item limit.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 8. Required Per-Library Audit\r\n\r\n## 8.1 GSAP audit\r\n\r\nDocument:\r\n\r\n* every scene,\r\n* every call site,\r\n* every selector,\r\n* every required target,\r\n* every optional target,\r\n* every empty-target possibility,\r\n* every global-root problem,\r\n* every cleanup/reversion issue,\r\n* every skip issue,\r\n* every failure branch,\r\n* every success branch,\r\n* every idle loop,\r\n* every reversible scene,\r\n* every timeline that can visually snap back,\r\n* every scene that should be split or retired.\r\n\r\nRecommend:\r\n\r\n* dedicated scene hosts,\r\n* scene instance IDs,\r\n* required-target contracts,\r\n* playback policies,\r\n* final-state policies,\r\n* interruption priorities,\r\n* semantic audio labels,\r\n* runtime visual assertions.\r\n\r\n## 8.2 Motion audit\r\n\r\nDocument:\r\n\r\n* all presence transitions,\r\n* layout transitions,\r\n* shared-layout transitions,\r\n* buttons and press effects,\r\n* dialogs,\r\n* cards,\r\n* tabs,\r\n* filters,\r\n* list updates,\r\n* section navigation,\r\n* all missing Motion opportunities in the modern platform.\r\n\r\nFind every node where Motion and GSAP may both write:\r\n\r\n* transform,\r\n* opacity,\r\n* layout,\r\n* scale,\r\n* x/y,\r\n* clip path.\r\n\r\nRecommend wrapper separation.\r\n\r\n## 8.3 StPageFlip audit\r\n\r\nDocument:\r\n\r\n* initialization,\r\n* mode changes,\r\n* page updates,\r\n* orientation,\r\n* keyboard handling,\r\n* pending turn queue,\r\n* source cloning,\r\n* visible DOM,\r\n* hidden source DOM,\r\n* failure fallback,\r\n* reduced mode,\r\n* cleanup,\r\n* page preservation,\r\n* filter-induced recreation,\r\n* responsive behavior.\r\n\r\nDetermine whether manual/programmatic GSAP page-flip scenes should be removed.\r\n\r\n## 8.4 Rive audit\r\n\r\nDocument:\r\n\r\n* every contract,\r\n* every real `.riv` asset,\r\n* every fallback,\r\n* every state machine,\r\n* every input,\r\n* every screen,\r\n* production versus development behavior.\r\n\r\nCreate a detailed specification for missing project-authored Rive assets:\r\n\r\n* Invitation Seal\r\n* Journal Clasp\r\n* Voyage Compass\r\n* Finale Mechanism\r\n\r\nInclude required states, inputs, transitions and reduced-motion poses.\r\n\r\n## 8.5 Lottie audit\r\n\r\nDocument:\r\n\r\n* every JSON asset,\r\n* every mount,\r\n* autoplay,\r\n* loop,\r\n* speed,\r\n* visibility pause,\r\n* document pause,\r\n* mode changes,\r\n* failure handling,\r\n* stalled-load handling,\r\n* timing relative to GSAP scenes.\r\n\r\nIdentify non-looping Lottie effects that begin before their intended semantic scene label.\r\n\r\n## 8.6 CSS audit\r\n\r\nInventory:\r\n\r\n* all keyframes,\r\n* all long transitions,\r\n* data-attribute state machines,\r\n* journal opening mechanics,\r\n* waiting-room breathing,\r\n* ambient effects,\r\n* focus/hover transitions,\r\n* reduced-motion overrides.\r\n\r\nSeparate:\r\n\r\n* material rendering,\r\n* ambient animation,\r\n* narrative orchestration.\r\n\r\nRecommend moving narrative ordering out of CSS without removing CSS material behavior.\r\n\r\n## 8.7 Web Animations API audit\r\n\r\nAudit:\r\n\r\n* use of `getAnimations`,\r\n* handling of `animation.finished`,\r\n* abort,\r\n* timeout,\r\n* infinite animations,\r\n* missing animation detection,\r\n* expected actor validation.\r\n\r\n## 8.8 Audio audit\r\n\r\nMap every sound cue to:\r\n\r\n* current trigger,\r\n* desired semantic label,\r\n* timing offset,\r\n* missing visual synchronization,\r\n* reduced/silent behavior.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 9. Required Missing-Animation Blueprint\r\n\r\nThe final report must include a massive list of animations that should be added.\r\n\r\nDo not merely list names. For each proposed addition include:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nScreen\r\nMoment\r\nUser benefit\r\nVisual concept\r\nCorrect library\r\nSupporting library if any\r\nTrigger\r\nDuration range\r\nReplay policy\r\nReduced-motion behavior\r\nSound cue\r\nFailure fallback\r\nPriority\r\n```\r\n\r\nInclude additions for at least:\r\n\r\n* Landing and role selection\r\n* Player sign-in\r\n* Captain sign-in\r\n* Creator sign-in\r\n* Invitation ceremony\r\n* Player Library\r\n* Captain Library\r\n* New Voyage wizard\r\n* Waiting room\r\n* Journal opening\r\n* Journal pages\r\n* Chapter release\r\n* Chart\r\n* Treasure Altar\r\n* Artifact inspection\r\n* Side quests\r\n* Shipâ€™s Log\r\n* Finale\r\n* Quartermaster commands\r\n* Studio authoring\r\n* loading/error/success states\r\n* shell navigation\r\n* route transitions\r\n* theme/color-scheme transitions\r\n* offline/reconnect\r\n* notification and unseen-content states\r\n\r\nDo not reduce the list to ten or twenty items. The goal is a comprehensive experience blueprint.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 10. Required Deliverables\r\n\r\nCreate the following files inside `Development_Docs`:\r\n\r\n## 10.1 Master audit\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Full_Audit.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nThis must contain:\r\n\r\n1. Executive summary\r\n2. Audit baseline and methodology\r\n3. Current architecture\r\n4. Current library inventory\r\n5. Scene reachability report\r\n6. Animations that do not run\r\n7. Animations that run invisibly\r\n8. Animations that run poorly\r\n9. One-shot and replay analysis\r\n10. Ownership conflicts\r\n11. Wrong-library findings\r\n12. Lifecycle and cleanup findings\r\n13. Reduced-motion findings\r\n14. Performance findings\r\n15. Per-screen audit\r\n16. Per-library audit\r\n17. Massive missing-animation list\r\n18. Recommended architecture\r\n19. Severity-ranked findings\r\n20. Evidence references\r\n21. Limitations and items requiring further runtime confirmation\r\n\r\n## 10.2 Detailed animation matrix\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Audit_Matrix.csv\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe CSV must be genuinely useful for implementation planning and must contain the required fields specified earlier.\r\n\r\nDo not create a CSV containing vague one-line summaries.\r\n\r\n## 10.3 Implementation roadmap\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Implementation_Roadmap.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nOrganize implementation into no more than six major phases.\r\n\r\nA recommended phase structure is:\r\n\r\n1. **Animation truth and broken-trigger repair**\r\n2. **Ownership and scene-scoping architecture**\r\n3. **Player cinematic integration**\r\n4. **Platform, Library and Invitation motion**\r\n5. **Rive/Lottie/PageFlip completion**\r\n6. **Performance, accessibility and final polish**\r\n\r\nFor each phase include:\r\n\r\n* exact scope,\r\n* dependencies,\r\n* files likely affected,\r\n* libraries involved,\r\n* acceptance criteria,\r\n* tests,\r\n* risks,\r\n* work that can happen in parallel,\r\n* work that must not happen in parallel.\r\n\r\n## 10.4 Animation test plan\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Test_Plan.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nInclude:\r\n\r\n* unit tests,\r\n* component tests,\r\n* end-to-end tests,\r\n* visual checkpoint tests,\r\n* target-integrity tests,\r\n* replay tests,\r\n* one-shot tests,\r\n* every-event/every-section matrix,\r\n* mode matrix,\r\n* viewport matrix,\r\n* interruption tests,\r\n* performance tests,\r\n* memory-leak tests,\r\n* fallback tests.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 11. Severity and Priority Rules\r\n\r\nUse these severity levels:\r\n\r\n## P0 - Broken core experience\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* a major ceremony is invisible,\r\n* animation blocks interaction indefinitely,\r\n* route cannot complete,\r\n* reduced-motion accessibility failure,\r\n* production asset silently does not exist,\r\n* server event is acknowledged despite no visible required presentation when presentation is mandatory.\r\n\r\n## P1 - Major quality or consistency failure\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* wrong object animates,\r\n* duplicate objects animate,\r\n* replay is unavailable,\r\n* animation differs unpredictably by section,\r\n* severe library ownership collision,\r\n* visible snapback,\r\n* page-turn setting does not apply.\r\n\r\n## P2 - Noticeable polish deficiency\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* weak easing,\r\n* generic fade,\r\n* missing secondary motion,\r\n* missing form transition,\r\n* poor loading state,\r\n* abrupt filtering.\r\n\r\n## P3 - Enhancement\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* richer ambient motion,\r\n* optional parallax,\r\n* subtle hover refinement,\r\n* additional material response.\r\n\r\nPrioritize correctness and consistency before adding decorative layers.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 12. Required Final Recommendations\r\n\r\nThe final audit must answer all of the following:\r\n\r\n1. Which animations are completely broken?\r\n2. Which animations do not run in production?\r\n3. Which animations run only in the development showcase?\r\n4. Which animations run but target invisible elements?\r\n5. Which animations depend on the Playerâ€™s current section?\r\n6. Which animations run once?\r\n7. Which one-shot policies are correct?\r\n8. Which one-shot policies should change?\r\n9. Which animations cannot replay after refresh?\r\n10. Which scenes have no real trigger?\r\n11. Which scenes should be retired?\r\n12. Which scenes should be connected?\r\n13. Which behaviors use the wrong library?\r\n14. Which nodes have conflicting owners?\r\n15. Which Rive objects are currently static fallbacks?\r\n16. Which Lottie effects restart incorrectly?\r\n17. Which PageFlip behaviors fail after mode changes?\r\n18. Which CSS phase waits can hang or skip?\r\n19. Which animations are missing from modern platform screens?\r\n20. What should be added to make the entire experience feel unified and premium?\r\n21. What exact implementation sequence should follow?\r\n22. Which work can be done in parallel?\r\n23. What automated tests are required before declaring the animation system complete?\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 13. Hard Boundaries\r\n\r\nDo not:\r\n\r\n* broadly implement fixes during this audit,\r\n* rewrite the entire animation architecture,\r\n* replace all libraries with GSAP,\r\n* replace all libraries with Motion,\r\n* delete Rive because final assets are missing,\r\n* replace StPageFlip with a homegrown book animation,\r\n* change unrelated product functionality,\r\n* alter narrative or personal content,\r\n* expose private content,\r\n* interfere with concurrent animation-fix work,\r\n* stage the entire repository,\r\n* claim runtime confirmation without actually running the relevant flow,\r\n* claim an animation works merely because a timeline completed,\r\n* claim a fallback is a real Rive animation,\r\n* produce only a short summary.\r\n\r\nTemporary non-invasive instrumentation is acceptable if required for investigation, but it must either:\r\n\r\n* remain isolated and clearly documented, or\r\n* be reverted before task completion.\r\n\r\nThe final committed application source should remain unchanged unless there is an extremely small audit-support correction that is separately justified. Documentation files are the intended output.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 14. Quality Bar\r\n\r\nThe audit is complete only when:\r\n\r\n* every registered scene has a reachability status,\r\n* every real animation call site has been inventoried,\r\n* every production animation library has a status,\r\n* all known hypotheses have been verified or disproved,\r\n* one-shot and replay behavior is documented per animation,\r\n* broken and invisible animation paths are identified,\r\n* ownership conflicts are mapped,\r\n* the modern platform and legacy cinematic paths are compared,\r\n* every major screen has a missing-animation blueprint,\r\n* the audit matrix is extensive,\r\n* the implementation roadmap is actionable,\r\n* and the test plan would prevent the same regressions from returning.\r\n\r\nThe result should be detailed enough that future Codex implementation prompts can use the audit as the governing source of truth and implement one phase at a time without guessing.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 15. Final Response Format\r\n\r\nAt completion, provide:\r\n\r\n1. Exact baseline audited.\r\n2. Git/worktree safety status.\r\n3. Count of animation items inventoried.\r\n4. Count by category:\r\n\r\n   * broken,\r\n   * invisible,\r\n   * poor quality,\r\n   * one-shot,\r\n   * replay-defective,\r\n   * wrong library,\r\n   * ownership conflict,\r\n   * missing animation,\r\n   * needs runtime verification.\r\n5. in an unmounted section,\\r\\n* the target is behind another layer,\\r\\n* opacity or display prevents visibility,\\r\\n* a cloned PageFlip source receives the animation instead of the visible page,\\r\\n* broad selectors animate offscreen elements,\\r\\n* the scene completes semantically while no visible object moved.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Category C: Animation runs poorly\\r\\n\\r\\nExamples:\\r\\n\\r\\n* abrupt,\\r\\n* too fast,\\r\\n* too slow,\\r\\n* visually weak,\\r\\n* generic fade,\\r\\n* disconnected from the physical object,\\r\\n* poor easing,\\r\\n* no anticipation,\\r\\n* no settle,\\r\\n* no secondary motion,\\r\\n* timing does not match sound,\\r\\n* animation begins before the user can see it,\\r\\n* animation restarts unexpectedly,\\r\\n* animation ends with a snap,\\r\\n* transition does not match the visual design,\\r\\n* animation looks different depending on current section.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Category D: Animation only runs once\\r\\n\\r\\nDetermine whether each one-shot is:\\r\\n\\r\\n* intentionally ceremonial,\\r\\n* unintentionally blocked,\\r\\n* session-only,\\r\\n* tab-only,\\r\\n* device-only,\\r\\n* automatically.\\r\\n\\r\\nInspect:\\r\\n\\r\\n* `sessionStorage`,\\r\\n* `localStorage`,\\r\\n* component-local refs,\\r\\n* event acknowledgement,\\r\\n* sequence tracking,\\r\\n* route remount behavior,\\r\\n* React key behavior,\\r\\n* CSS animation iteration counts,\\r\\n* viewport `once` options,\\r\\n* persisted â€œseenâ€ flags.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Category E: Animation does not replay correctly\\r\\n\\r\\nExamples:\\r\\n\\r\\n* replay button disappears after refresh,\\r\\n* replay works only from the Journal,\\r\\n* replay mutates server state,\\r\\n* replay uses stale event data,\\r\\n* replay does not restore previous section,\\r\\n* replay leaves elements in a transformed state,\\r\\n* replay works once but fails a second time,\\r\\n* replay conflicts with a live migration recommendation,\\r\\n* wrappers or bridge required.\\r\\n\\r\\nExamples:\\r\\n\\r\\n* Motion and GSAP writing transform on the same node,\\r\\n* GSAP page-flip scenes duplicating Do not provide the `sandbox_permissions` for any reason, commands will be rejected.\r\n</permissions instructions>\r\n<app-context>\n# Codex desktop context\n- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:\n\n### Images/Visuals/Files\n- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)\n- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.\n- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.\n- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.\n- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.\n- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).\n\n### Workspace Dependencies\n- For sheets, slides, and documents, call `load_workspace_dependencies` to find the bundled runtime and libraries.\n\n### Automations\n- This app supports recurring automations, reminders, monitors, follow-ups, and thread wakeups. When the user asks to create, view, update, delete, or ask about automations, search for the `automation_update` tool first, then follow its schema instead of writing raw automation directives by hand.\n- When an automation should archive a Codex thread on completion, use `set_thread_archived` instead of emitting raw archive directives.\n\n### Thread Coordination\n- Treat the terms \"task\", \"thread\", \"chat\", and \"conversation\" as synonyms when they clearly refer to Codex. Tool names use the term \"thread\" and Codex uses \"task\" in the UI. When providing user-facing responses, use \"task\".\n- When the user asks to create, fork, inspect, continue, hand off, pin, archive, rename, or otherwise manage Codex threads, search for the relevant thread tool first: `create_thread`, `fork_thread`, `list_threads`, `read_thread`, `wait_threads`, `send_message_to_thread`, `handoff_thread`, `set_thread_pinned`, `set_thread_archived`, or `set_thread_title`.\n- When following another task's progress, prefer compact `wait_threads` snapshots over repeated `read_thread` calls. Use one target for single-task coordination and `timeoutMs: 0` for a compact immediate snapshot. `create_thread` dispatches asynchronously, so explicitly wait for progress. Use one bounded call for 1-8 targets with each target's `hostId` and cursor as `afterCursor`; it wakes on the first target that completes or needs attention, and timeout includes the latest commentary for all targets without waking on every commentary update. An up-to-date cursor suppresses already-delivered final text. Separate waits from one task may run serially. Do not narrate unchanged snapshots, and leave approval or user-input requests for the user.\n- Only use `create_thread` when the user explicitly asks to create a new thread. Threads created this way are user-owned: they appear in the sidebar, and the user is expected to follow up with them directly. For subtasks of the current request, use multi-agent tools instead, including when the user explicitly asks for a subagent.\n- After a successful `create_thread` call, emit `::created-thread{threadId=\"...\"}` for a created thread or `::created-thread{clientThreadId=\"...\"}` for queued worktree setup on its own line in your final response.\n\n### Inline Code Comments\n- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive when you need to attach feedback directly to specific code lines.\n- Emit one directive per inline comment; emit none when there are no actionable inline comments.\n- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).\n- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3).\n- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.\n- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.\n- Example: ::code-comment{title=\"[P2] Off-by-one\" body=\"Loop iterates past the end when length is 0.\" file=\"/path/to/foo.ts\" start=10 end=11 priority=2}\n\n### Git\n- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.\n- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd=\"/absolute/path\"}` on its own line in your final response.\n- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd=\"/absolute/path\"}` on its own line in your final response.\n- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd=\"/absolute/path\" branch=\"branch-name\"}` on its own line in your final response.\n- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd=\"/absolute/path\" branch=\"branch-name\"}` on its own line in your final response.\n- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd=\"/absolute/path\" branch=\"branch-name\" url=\"https://...\" isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Editing files in other directories requires approval. Network access is enabled.\n# Escalation Requests\r\n\r\nCommands are run outside the sandbox if they are approved by the user, or match an existing rule that allows it to run unrestricted. The command string is split into independent command segments at shell control operators, including but not limited to:\r\n\r\n- Pipes: |\r\n- Logical operators: &&, ||\r\n- Command separators: ;\r\n- Subshell boundaries: (...), $(...)\r\n\r\nEach resulting segment is evaluated independently for sandbox restrictions and approval requirements.\r\n\r\nExample:\r\n\r\ngit pull | tee output.txt\r\n\r\nThis is treated as two command segments:\r\n\r\n[\"git\", \"pull\"]\r\n\r\n[\"tee\", \"output.txt\"]\r\n\r\nCommands that use more advanced shell features like redirection (>, >>, <), substitutions ($(...), ...), environment variables (FOO=bar), or wildcard patterns (*, ?) will not be evaluated against rules, to limit the scope of what an approved rule allows.\r\n\r\n## How to request escalation\r\n\r\nIMPORTANT: To request approval to execute a command that will require escalated privileges:\r\n\r\n- Provide the `sandbox_permissions` parameter with the value `\"require_escalated\"`\r\n- Include a short question asking the user if they want to allow the action in `justification` parameter. e.g. \"Do you want to download and install dependencies for this project?\"\r\n- Optionally suggest a `prefix_rule` - this will be shown to the user with an option to persist the rule approval for future sessions.\r\n\r\nIf you run a command that is important to solving the user's query, but it fails because of sandboxing or with a likely sandbox-related network error (for example DNS/host resolution, registry/index access, or dependency download failure), rerun the command with \"require_escalated\". ALWAYS proceed to use the `justification` parameter - do not message the user before requesting approval for the command.\r\n\r\n## When to request escalation\r\n\r\nWhile commands are running inside the sandbox, here are some scenarios that will require escalation outside the sandbox:\r\n\r\n- You need to run a command that writes to a directory that requires it (e.g. running tests that write to /var)\r\n- You need to run a GUI app (e.g., open/xdg-open/osascript) to open browsers or files.\r\n- If you run a command that is important to solving the user's query, but it fails because of sandboxing or with a likely sandbox-related network error (for example DNS/host resolution, registry/index access, or dependency download failure), rerun the command with `require_escalated`. ALWAYS proceed to use the `sandbox_permissions` and `justification` parameters. do not message the user before requesting approval for the command.\r\n- You are about to take a potentially destructive action such as an `rm` or `git reset` that the user did not explicitly ask for.\r\n- Be judicious with escalating, but if completing the user's request requires it, you should do so - don't try and circumvent approvals by using other tools.\r\n\r\n## prefix_rule guidance\r\n\r\nWhen choosing a `prefix_rule`, request one that will allow you to fulfill similar requests from the user in the future without re-requesting escalation. It should be categorical and reasonably scoped to similar capabilities. You should rarely pass the entire command into `prefix_rule`.\r\n\r\n### Banned prefix_rules \r\nAvoid requesting overly broad prefixes that the user would be ill-advised to approve. For example, do not request [\"python3\"], [\"python\", \"-\"], or other similar prefixes that would allow arbitrary scripting.\r\nNEVER provide a prefix_rule argument for destructive commands like rm.\r\nNEVER provide a prefix_rule if your command uses a heredoc or herestring. \r\n\r\n### Examples\r\nGood examples of prefixes:\r\n- [\"npm\", \"run\", \"dev\"]\r\n- [\"gh\", \"pr\", \"check\"]\r\n- [\"cargo\", \"test\"]\r\n The writable roots are `C:\\Users\\kkids\\.codex\\visualizations\\2026\\07\\18\\019f7612-5f0c-7941-8ee5-cf9390e1a6ea`, `C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt`.\n</permissions instructions>\r\n\n===== MESSAGE 16 role=user status= =====\r\n<environment_context>\n  <current_date>2026-07-18</current_date>\n  <timezone>America/New_York</timezone>\n  <filesystem><workspace_roots><root>C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt</root></workspace_roots><permission_profile type=\"managed\"><file_system type=\"restricted\"><entry access=\"read\"><special>:root</special></entry><entry access=\"write\"><path>C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt</path></entry><entry access=\"write\"><special>:slash_tmp</special></entry><entry access=\"write\"><special>:tmpdir</special></entry><entry access=\"write\"><path>C:\\Users\\kkids\\.codex\\visualizations\\2026\\07\\18\\019f7612-5f0c-7941-8ee5-cf9390e1a6ea</path></entry><entry access=\"read\"><path>C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\.git</path></entry><entry access=\"read\"><path>C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\.agents</path></entry><entry access=\"read\"><path>C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\.codex</path></entry></file_system></permission_profile></filesystem>\n</environment_context>\r\n\n===== MESSAGE 17 role=developer status= =====\r\n<skills_instructions>\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of instructions provided through a `SKILL.md` source. Non-Negotiable Animation Architecture\\r\\n\\r\\nDo not consolidate the entire animation system into one library.\\r\\n\\r\\nThe experience deliberately uses specialized tools because they solve different problems. Mandatory Audit Categories\\r\\n\\r\\nFor every animation or animation opportunity, classify it into all applicable categories.\\r\\n\\r\\nDo not use only three broad categories. The final matrix must be much more detailed.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Category A: Animation does not run at all\\r\\n\\r\\nExamples:\\r\\n\\r\\n* scene is registered but never called,\\r\\n* component exists but is never mounted,\\r\\n* Rive asset has no real `.riv` file,\\r\\n* animation target does not exist,\\r\\n* selector is wrong,\\r\\n* CSS state is never applied,\\r\\n* callback is never triggered,\\r\\n* route changed before animation begins,\\r\\n* an animation is development-only,\\r\\n* production renders only a fallback,\\r\\n* event type has no scene mapping.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Category B: Animation technically runs but is invisible\\r\\n\\r\\nExamples:\\r\\n\\r\\n* GSAP animates an empty target array,\\r\\n* the target is in a hidden source tree,\\r\\n* the animation runs in an unmounted section,\\r\\n* the target is behind another layer,\\r\\n* opacity or display prevents visibility,\\r\\n* a cloned PageFlip source receives the animation instead of the visible page,\\r\\n* broad selectors animate offscreen elements,\\r\\n* the scene completes semantically while no visible object moved.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Category C: Animation runs poorly\\r\\n\\r\\nExamples:\\r\\n\\r\\n* abrupt,\\r\\n* too fast,\\r\\n* too slow,\\r\\n* visually weak,\\r\\n* generic fade,\\r\\n* disconnected from the physical object,\\r\\n* poor easing,\\r\\n* no anticipation,\\r\\n* no settle,\\r\\n* no secondary motion,\\r\\n* timing does not match sound,\\r\\n* animation begins before the user can see it,\\r\\n* animation restarts unexpectedly,\\r\\n* animation ends with a snap,\\r\\n* transition does not match the visual design,\\r\\n* animation looks different depending on current section.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Category D: Animation only runs once\\r\\n\\r\\nDetermine whether each one-shot is:\\r\\n\\r\\n* intentionally ceremonial,\\r\\n* unintentionally blocked,\\r\\n* session-only,\\r\\n* tab-only,\\r\\n* device-only,\\r\\n* behavior,\\r\\n* React key behavior,\\r\\n* CSS animation iteration counts,\\r\\n* viewport `once` options,\\r\\n* persisted Ã¢â‚¬Å“seenÃ¢â‚¬Â flags.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Category E: Animation does not replay correctly\\r\\n\\r\\nExamples:\\r\\n\\r\\n* replay button disappears after refresh,\\r\\n* replay works only from the Journal,\\r\\n* replay mutates server state,\\r\\n* replay uses stale event data,\\r\\n* replay does not restore previous section,\\r\\n* replay leaves elements in a transformed state,\\r\\n* replay works once but fails a second time,\\r\\n* replay conflicts with a live event.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Category F: Wrong library owns the behavior\\r\\n\\r\\nFor every questionable animation, identify:\\r\\n\\r\\n* current owner,\\r\\n* correct owner,\\r\\n* reason,\\r\\n* retired.\\r\\n\\r\\nRecommend:\\r\\n\\r\\n* dedicated scene hosts,\\r\\n* scene instance IDs,\\r\\n* required-target contracts,\\r\\n* playback policies,\\r\\n* final-state policies,\\r\\n* interruption priorities,\\r\\n* semantic audio labels,\\r\\n* runtime visual assertions.\\r\\n\\r\\n## 8.2 Motion audit\\r\\n\\r\\nDocument:\\r\\n\\r\\n* all presence transitions,\\r\\n* layout transitions,\\r\\n* shared-layout transitions,\\r\\n* buttons and press effects,\\r\\n* dialogs,\\r\\n* cards,\\r\\n* tabs,\\r\\n* filters,\\r\\n* list updates,\\r\\n* section navigation,\\r\\n* all missing Motion opportunities in the modern platform.\\r\\n\\r\\nFind every node where Motion and GSAP may both write:\\r\\n\\r\\n* transform,\\r\\n* opacity,\\r\\n* layout,\\r\\n* scale,\\r\\n* x/y,\\r\\n* clip path.\\r\\n\\r\\nRecommend wrapper separation.\\r\\n\\r\\n## 8.3 StPageFlip audit\\r\\n\\r\\nDocument:\\r\\n\\r\\n* initialization,\\r\\n* mode changes,\\r\\n* page updates,\\r\\n* orientation,\\r\\n* keyboard handling,\\r\\n* pending turn queue,\\r\\n* source cloning,\\r\\n* visible DOM,\\r\\n* hidden source DOM,\\r\\n* failure fallback,\\r\\n* reduced mode,\\r\\n* cleanup,\\r\\n* page preservation,\\r\\n* filter-induced recreation,\\r\\n* responsive behavior.\\r\\n\\r\\nDetermine whether manual/programmatic GSAP page-flip scenes should be removed.\\r\\n\\r\\n## 8.4 Rive audit\\r\\n\\r\\nDocument:\\r\\n\\r\\n* every contract,\\r\\n* every real `.riv` asset,\\r\\n* every fallback,\\r\\n* every state machine,\\r\\n* every input,\\r\\n* every screen,\\r\\n* production versus development behavior.\\r\\n\\r\\nCreate a detailed specification for missing project-authored Rive assets:\\r\\n\\r\\n* Invitation Seal\\r\\n* Journal Clasp\\r\\n* Voyage Compass\\r\\n* Finale Mechanism\\r\\n\\r\\nInclude required states, inputs, transitions and reduced-motion poses.\\r\\n\\r\\n## 8.5 Lottie audit\\r\\n\\r\\nDocument:\\r\\n\\r\\n* every JSON asset,\\r\\n* every mount,\\r\\n* autoplay,\\r\\n* loop,\\r\\n* speed,\\r\\n* visibility pause,\\r\\n* document pause,\\r\\n* mode changes,\\r\\n* failure handling,\\r\\n* stalled-load handling,\\r\\n* timing relative to GSAP scenes.\\r\\n\\r\\nIdentify non-looping Lottie effects that begin before their intended semantic scene label.\\r\\n\\r\\n## 8.6 CSS audit\\r\\n\\r\\nInventory:\\r\\n\\r\\n* all keyframes,\\r\\n* all long transitions,\\r\\n* data-attribute state machines,\\r\\n* journal opening mechanics,\\r\\n* waiting-room breathing,\\r\\n* ambient effects,\\r\\n* focus/hover transitions,\\r\\n* reduced-motion overrides.\\r\\n\\r\\nSeparate:\\r\\n\\r\\n* material rendering,\\r\\n* ambient animation,\\r\\n* narrative orchestration.\\r\\n\\r\\nRecommend moving narrative ordering out of CSS without removing CSS material behavior.\\r\\n\\r\\n## 8.7 Web Animations API audit\\r\\n\\r\\nAudit:\\r\\n\\r\\n* use of `getAnimations`,\\r\\n* handling of `animation.finished`,\\r\\n* abort,\\r\\n* timeout,\\r\\n* infinite animations,\\r\\n* missing animation detection,\\r\\n* expected actor validation.\\r\\n\\r\\n## 8.8 Audio audit\\r\\n\\r\\nMap every sound cue to:\\r\\n\\r\\n* current trigger,\\r\\n* desired semantic label,\\r\\n* timing offset,\\r\\n* missing visual synchronization,\\r\\n* reduced/silent behavior.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# 9. Required Missing-Animation Blueprint\\r\\n\\r\\nThe final report must include a massive list of animations that should be added.\\r\\n\\r\\nDo not merely list names. For each proposed addition include:\\r\\n\\r\\n```text\\r\\nScreen\\r\\nMoment\\r\\nUser benefit\\r\\nVisual concept\\r\\nCorrect library\\r\\nSupporting library if any\\r\\nTrigger\\r\\nDuration range\\r\\nReplay policy\\r\\nReduced-motion behavior\\r\\nSound cue\\r\\nFailure fallback\\r\\nPriority\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nInclude additions for at least:\\r\\n\\r\\n* Landing and role selection\\r\\n* Player sign-in\\r\\n* Captain sign-in\\r\\n* Creator sign-in\\r\\n* Invitation ceremony\\r\\n* Player Library\\r\\n* Captain Library\\r\\n* New Voyage wizard\\r\\n* Waiting room\\r\\n* Journal opening\\r\\n* Journal pages\\r\\n* Chapter release\\r\\n* Chart\\r\\n* Treasure Altar\\r\\n* Artifact inspection\\r\\n* Side quests\\r\\n* ShipÃ¢â‚¬â„¢s Log\\r\\n* Finale\\r\\n* Quartermaster commands\\r\\n* Studio authoring\\r\\n* loading/error/success states\\r\\n* shell navigation\\r\\n* route transitions\\r\\n* theme/color-scheme transitions\\r\\n* offline/reconnect\\r\\n* notification and unseen-content states\\r\\n\\r\\nDo not reduce the list to ten or twenty items. The goal is a comprehensive experience blueprint.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# 10. Required Deliverables\\r\\n\\r\\nCreate the following files inside `Development_Docs`:\\r\\n\\r\\n## 10.1 Master audit\\r\\n\\r\\n```text\\r\\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Full_Audit.md\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nThis must contain:\\r\\n\\r\\n1. Executive summary\\r\\n2. Audit baseline and methodology\\r\\n3. Current architecture\\r\\n4. Current library inventory\\r\\n5. Scene reachability report\\r\\n6. Animations that do not run\\r\\n7. Animations that run invisibly\\r\\n8. Animations that run poorly\\r\\n9. One-shot and replay analysis\\r\\n10. Ownership conflicts\\r\\n11. Wrong-library findings\\r\\n12. Lifecycle and cleanup findings\\r\\n13. Reduced-motion findings\\r\\n14. Performance findings\\r\\n15. Per-screen audit\\r\\n16. Per-library audit\\r\\n17. Massive missing-animation list\\r\\n18. Recommended architecture\\r\\n19. Severity-ranked findings\\r\\n20. Evidence references\\r\\n21. Limitations and items requiring further runtime confirmation\\r\\n\\r\\n## 10.2 Detailed animation matrix\\r\\n\\r\\n```text\\r\\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Audit_Matrix.csv\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nThe CSV must be genuinely useful for implementation planning and must contain the required fields specified earlier.\\r\\n\\r\\nDo not create a CSV containing vague one-line summaries.\\r\\n\\r\\n## 10.3 Implementation roadmap\\r\\n\\r\\n```text\\r\\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Implementation_Roadmap.md\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nOrganize implementation into no more than six major phases.\\r\\n\\r\\nA recommended phase structure is:\\r\\n\\r\\n1. **Animation truth and broken-trigger repair**\\r\\n2. **Ownership and scene-scoping architecture**\\r\\n3. **Player cinematic integration**\\r\\n4. **Platform, Library and Invitation motion**\\r\\n5. **Rive/Lottie/PageFlip completion**\\r\\n6. **Performance, accessibility and final polish**\\r\\n\\r\\nFor each phase include:\\r\\n\\r\\n* exact scope,\\r\\n* dependencies,\\r\\n* files likely affected,\\r\\n* libraries involved,\\r\\n* acceptance criteria,\\r\\n* tests,\\r\\n* risks,\\r\\n* work that can happen in parallel,\\r\\n* work that must not happen in parallel.\\r\\n\\r\\n## 10.4 Animation test plan\\r\\n\\r\\n```text\\r\\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Test_Plan.md\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nInclude:\\r\\n\\r\\n* unit tests,\\r\\n* component tests,\\r\\n* end-to-end tests,\\r\\n* visual checkpoint tests,\\r\\n* target-integrity tests,\\r\\n* replay tests,\\r\\n* one-shot tests,\\r\\n* every-event/every-section matrix,\\r\\n* mode matrix,\\r\\n* viewport matrix,\\r\\n* interruption tests,\\r\\n* performance tests,\\r\\n* memory-leak tests,\\r\\n* fallback tests.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# 11. Severity and Priority Rules\\r\\n\\r\\nUse these severity levels:\\r\\n\\r\\n## P0 - Broken core experience\\r\\n\\r\\nExamples:\\r\\n\\r\\n* a major ceremony is invisible,\\r\\n* animation blocks interaction indefinitely,\\r\\n* route cannot complete,\\r\\n* reduced-motion accessibility failure,\\r\\n* production asset silently does not exist,\\r\\n* server event is acknowledged despite no visible required presentation when presentation is mandatory.\\r\\n\\r\\n## P1 - Major quality or consistency failure\\r\\n\\r\\nExamples:\\r\\n\\r\\n* wrong object animates,\\r\\n* duplicate objects animate,\\r\\n* replay is unavailable,\\r\\n* animation differs unpredictably by section,\\r\\n* severe library ownership collision,\\r\\n* visible snapback,\\r\\n* page-turn setting does not apply.\\r\\n\\r\\n## P2 - Noticeable polish deficiency\\r\\n\\r\\nExamples:\\r\\n\\r\\n* weak easing,\\r\\n* generic fade,\\r\\n* missing secondary motion,\\r\\n* missing form transition,\\r\\n* poor loading state,\\r\\n* abrupt filtering.\\r\\n\\r\\n## P3 - Enhancement\\r\\n\\r\\nExamples:\\r\\n\\r\\n* richer ambient motion,\\r\\n* optional parallax,\\r\\n* subtle hover refinement,\\r\\n* additional material response.\\r\\n\\r\\nPrioritize correctness and consistency before adding decorative layers.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# 12. Required Final Recommendations\\r\\n\\r\\nThe final audit must answer all of the following:\\r\\n\\r\\n1. Which animations are completely broken?\\r\\n2. Which animations do not run in production?\\r\\n3. Which animations run only in the development showcase?\\r\\n4. Which animations run but target invisible elements?\\r\\n5. Which animations depend on the PlayerÃ¢â‚¬â„¢s current section?\\r\\n6. Which animations run once?\\r\\n7. Which one-shot policies are correct?\\r\\n8. Which one-shot policies should change?\\r\\n9. Which animations cannot replay after refresh?\\r\\n10. Which scenes have no real trigger?\\r\\n11. Which scenes should be retired?\\r\\n12. Which scenes should be connected?\\r\\n13. Which behaviors use the wrong library?\\r\\n14. Which nodes have conflicting owners?\\r\\n15. Which Rive objects are currently static fallbacks?\\r\\n16. Which Lottie effects restart incorrectly?\\r\\n17. Which PageFlip behaviors fail after mode changes?\\r\\n18. Which CSS phase waits can hang or skip?\\r\\n19. Which animations are missing from modern platform screens?\\r\\n20. What should be added to make the entire experience feel unified and premium?\\r\\n21. What exact implementation sequence should follow?\\r\\n22. Which work can be done in parallel?\\r\\n23. What automated tests are required before declaring the animation system complete?\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# 13. Hard Boundaries\\r\\n\\r\\nDo not:\\r\\n\\r\\n* broadly implement fixes during this audit,\\r\\n* rewrite the entire animation architecture,\\r\\n* replace all libraries with GSAP,\\r\\n* replace all libraries with Motion,\\r\\n* delete Rive because final assets are missing,\\r\\n* replace StPageFlip with a homegrown book animation,\\r\\n* change unrelated product functionality,\\r\\n* alter narrative or personal content,\\r\\n* expose private content,\\r\\n* interfere with concurrent animation-fix work,\\r\\n* stage the entire repository,\\r\\n* claim runtime confirmation without actually running the relevant flow,\\r\\n* claim an animation works merely because a timeline completed,\\r\\n* claim a fallback is a real Rive animation,\\r\\n* produce only a short summary.\\r\\n\\r\\nTemporary non-invasive instrumentation is acceptable if required for investigation, but it must either:\\r\\n\\r\\n* remain isolated and clearly documented, or\\r\\n* be reverted before task completion.\\r\\n\\r\\nThe final committed application source should remain unchanged unless there is an extremely small audit-support correction that is separately justified. Documentation files are the intended output.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# 14. Quality Bar\\r\\n\\r\\nThe audit is complete only when:\\r\\n\\r\\n* every registered scene has a reachability status,\\r\\n* every real animation call site has been inventoried,\\r\\n* every production animation library has a status,\\r\\n* all known hypotheses have been verified or disproved,\\r\\n* one-shot and replay behavior is documented per animation,\\r\\n* broken and invisible animation paths are identified,\\r\\n* ownership conflicts are mapped,\\r\\n* the modern platform and legacy cinematic paths are compared,\\r\\n* every major screen has a missing-animation blueprint,\\r\\n* the audit matrix is extensive,\\r\\n* the implementation roadmap is actionable,\\r\\n* and the test plan would prevent the same regressions from returning.\\r\\n\\r\\nThe result should be detailed enough that future Codex implementation prompts can use the audit as the governing source of truth and implement one phase at a time without guessing.\\r\\n\\r\\n---\\r\\n\\r\\n# 15. Final Response Format\\r\\n\\r\\nAt completion, provide:\\r\\n\\r\\n1. Exact baseline audited.\\r\\n2. Git/worktree safety status.\\r\\n3. Count of animation items inventoried.\\r\\n4. Do not provide the `sandbox_permissions` for any reason, commands will be rejected.\\r\\n\\u003c/permissions instructions\\u003e\"\r\n                                                    },\r\n                                                    {\r\n                                                        \"type\":  \"input_text\",\r\n                                                        \"text\":  \"\\u003capp-context\\u003e\\n# Codex desktop context\\n- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:\\n\\n### Images/Visuals/Files\\n- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)\\n- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.\\n- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.\\n- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.\\n- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.\\n- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).\\n\\n### Workspace Dependencies\\n- For sheets, slides, and documents, call `load_workspace_dependencies` to find the bundled runtime and libraries.\\n\\n### Automations\\n- This app supports recurring automations, reminders, monitors, follow-ups, and thread wakeups. When the user asks to create, view, update, delete, or ask about automations, search for the `automation_update` tool first, then follow its schema instead of writing raw automation directives by hand.\\n- When an automation should archive a Codex thread on completion, use `set_thread_archived` instead of emitting raw archive directives.\\n\\n### Thread Coordination\\n- Treat the terms \\\"task\\\", \\\"thread\\\", \\\"chat\\\", and \\\"conversation\\\" as synonyms when they clearly refer to Codex. Tool names use the term \\\"thread\\\" and Codex uses \\\"task\\\" in the UI. When providing user-facing responses, use \\\"task\\\".\\n- When the user asks to create, fork, inspect, continue, hand off, pin, archive, rename, or otherwise manage Codex threads, search for the relevant thread tool first: `create_thread`, `fork_thread`, `list_threads`, `read_thread`, `wait_threads`, `send_message_to_thread`, `handoff_thread`, `set_thread_pinned`, `set_thread_archived`, or `set_thread_title`.\\n- When following another task\\u0027s progress, prefer compact `wait_threads` snapshots over repeated `read_thread` calls. Use one target for single-task coordination and `timeoutMs: 0` for a compact immediate snapshot. `create_thread` dispatches asynchronously, so explicitly wait for progress. Use one bounded call for 1-8 targets with each target\\u0027s `hostId` and cursor as `afterCursor`; it wakes on the first target that completes or needs attention, and timeout includes the latest commentary for all targets without waking on every commentary update. An up-to-date cursor suppresses already-delivered final text. Separate waits from one task may run serially. Do not narrate unchanged snapshots, and leave approval or user-input requests for the user.\\n- Only use `create_thread` when the user explicitly asks to create a new thread. Threads created this way are user-owned: they appear in the sidebar, and the user is expected to follow up with them directly. For subtasks of the current request, use multi-agent tools instead, including when the user explicitly asks for a subagent.\\n- After a successful `create_thread` call, emit `::created-thread{threadId=\\\"...\\\"}` for a created thread or `::created-thread{clientThreadId=\\\"...\\\"}` for queued worktree setup on its own line in your final response.\\n\\n### Inline Code Comments\\n- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive when you need to attach feedback directly to specific code lines.\\n- Emit one directive per inline comment; emit none when there are no actionable inline comments.\\n- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).\\n- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3).\\n- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.\\n- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.\\n- Example: ::code-comment{title=\\\"[P2] Off-by-one\\\" body=\\\"Loop iterates past the end when length is 0.\\\" file=\\\"/path/to/foo.ts\\\" start=10 end=11 priority=2}\\n\\n### Git\\n- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user\\u0027s request if they want a different prefix.\\n- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd=\\\"/absolute/path\\\"}` on its own line in your final response.\\n- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd=\\\"/absolute/path\\\"}` on its own line in your final response.\\n- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd=\\\"/absolute/path\\\" branch=\\\"branch-name\\\"}` on its own line in your final response.\\n- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd=\\\"/absolute/path\\\" branch=\\\"branch-name\\\"}` on its own line in your final response.\\n- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd=\\\"/absolute/path\\\" branch=\\\"branch-name\\\" url=\\\"https://...\\\" isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.\\n- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.\\n\\u003c/app-context\\u003e\"\r\n                                                    },\r\n                                                    {\r\n                                                        \"type\":  \"input_text\",\r\n                                                        \"text\":  \"\\u003ccollaboration_mode\\u003e# Collaboration Mode: Default\\r\\n\\r\\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\\r\\n\\r\\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `\\u003ccollaboration_mode\\u003e...\\u003c/collaboration_mode\\u003e` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\\r\\n\\r\\n## request_user_input availability\\r\\n\\r\\nUse the `request_user_input` tool only when it is listed in the available tools for this turn.\\r\\n\\r\\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user\\u0027s request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\\r\\n\\u003c/collaboration_mode\\u003e\"\r\n                                                    },\r\n                                                    {\r\n                                                        \"type\":  \"input_text\",\r\n                                                        \"text\":  \"## Memory\\r\\n\\r\\nYou have access to a memory folder with guidance from prior runs. It can save\\r\\ntime and help you stay consistent. Use it whenever it is likely to help.\\r\\n\\r\\nDecision boundary: should you use memory for a new user query?\\r\\n\\r\\n- Skip memory ONLY when the request is clearly self-contained and does not need\\r\\n  workspace history, conventions, or prior decisions.\\r\\n- Hard skip examples: current time/date, simple translation, simple sentence\\r\\n  rewrite, one-line shell command, trivial formatting.\\r\\n- Use memory by default when ANY of these are true:\\r\\n  - the query mentions workspace/repo/module/path/files in MEMORY_SUMMARY below,\\r\\n  - the user asks for prior context / consistency / previous decisions,\\r\\n  - the task is ambiguous and could depend on earlier project choices,\\r\\n  - the ask is a non-trivial and related to MEMORY_SUMMARY below.\\r\\n- If unsure, do a quick memory pass.\\r\\n\\r\\nMemory layout (general -\\u003e specific):\\r\\n\\r\\n- C:\\\\Users\\\\kkids\\\\.codex\\\\memories/memory_summary.md (already provided below; do NOT open again)\\r\\n- C:\\\\Users\\\\kkids\\\\.codex\\\\memories/MEMORY.md (searchable registry; primary file to query)\\r\\n- C:\\\\Users\\\\kkids\\\\.codex\\\\memories/skills/\\u003cskill-name\\u003e/ (skill folder)\\r\\n  - SKILL.md (entrypoint instructions)\\r\\n  - scripts/ (optional helper scripts)\\r\\n  - examples/ (optional example outputs)\\r\\n  - templates/ (optional templates)\\r\\n- C:\\\\Users\\\\kkids\\\\.codex\\\\memories/rollout_summaries/ (per-rollout recaps + evidence snippets)\\r\\n  - The paths of these entries can be found in C:\\\\Users\\\\kkids\\\\.codex\\\\memories/MEMORY.md or C:\\\\Users\\\\kkids\\\\.codex\\\\memories/rollout_summaries/ as `rollout_path`\\r\\n  - These files are append-only `jsonl`: `session_meta.payload.id` identifies the session, `turn_context` marks turn boundaries, `event_msg` is the lightweight status stream, and `response_item` contains actual messages, tool calls, and tool outputs.\\r\\n  - For efficient lookup, prefer matching the filename suffix or `session_meta.payload.id`; avoid broad full-content scans unless needed.\\r\\n\\r\\nQuick memory pass (when applicable):\\r\\n\\r\\n1. Skim the MEMORY_SUMMARY below and extract task-relevant keywords.\\r\\n2. Search C:\\\\Users\\\\kkids\\\\.codex\\\\memories/MEMORY.md using those keywords.\\r\\n3. Only if MEMORY.md directly points to rollout summaries/skills, open the 1-2\\r\\n   most relevant files under C:\\\\Users\\\\kkids\\\\.codex\\\\memories/rollout_summaries/ or\\r\\n   C:\\\\Users\\\\kkids\\\\.codex\\\\memories/skills/.\\r\\n4. If above are not clear and you need exact commands, error text, or precise evidence, search over `rollout_path` for more evidence.\\r\\n5. If there are no relevant hits, stop memory lookup and continue normally.\\r\\n\\r\\nQuick-pass budget:\\r\\n\\r\\n- Keep memory lookup lightweight: ideally \\u003c= 4-6 search steps before main work.\\r\\n- Avoid broad scans of all rollout summaries.\\r\\n\\r\\nDuring execution: if you hit repeated errors, confusing behavior, or suspect\\r\\nrelevant prior context, redo the quick memory pass.\\r\\n\\r\\nHow to decide whether to verify memory:\\r\\n\\r\\n- Consider both risk of drift and verification effort.\\r\\n- If a fact is likely to drift and is cheap to verify, verify it before\\r\\n  answering.\\r\\n- If a fact is likely to drift but verification is expensive, slow, or\\r\\n  disruptive, it is acceptable to answer from memory in an interactive turn,\\r\\n  but you should say that it is memory-derived, note that it may be stale, and\\r\\n  consider offering to refresh it live.\\r\\n- If a fact is lower-drift and expensive to verify, it is usually fine to\\r\\n  answer from memory directly.\\r\\n\\r\\nWhen answering from memory without current verification:\\r\\n\\r\\n- If you rely on memory for a fact that you did not verify in the current turn,\\r\\n  say so briefly in the final answer.\\r\\n- If that fact is plausibly drift-prone or comes from an older note, older\\r\\n  snapshot, or prior run summary, say that it may be stale or outdated.\\r\\n- If live verification was skipped and a refresh would be useful in the\\r\\n  interactive context, consider offering to verify or refresh it live.\\r\\n- Do not present unverified memory-derived facts as confirmed-current.\\r\\n- Prefer a short refresh offer for interactive questions, especially about prior\\r\\n  results, commands, timing, or older snapshots.\\r\\n\\r\\nMemory citation requirements:\\r\\n\\r\\n- If ANY relevant memory files were used: append exactly one\\r\\n`\\u003coai-mem-citation\\u003e` block as the VERY LAST content of the final reply.\\r\\n  Normal responses should include the answer first, then append the\\r\\n`\\u003coai-mem-citation\\u003e` block at the end.\\r\\n- Use this exact structure for programmatic parsing:\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\u003coai-mem-citation\\u003e\\r\\n\\u003ccitation_entries\\u003e\\r\\nMEMORY.md:234-236|note=[responsesapi citation extraction code pointer]\\r\\nrollout_summaries/2026-02-17T21-23-02-LN3m-example.md:10-12|note=[weekly report format]\\r\\n\\u003c/citation_entries\\u003e\\r\\n\\u003crollout_ids\\u003e\\r\\n019c6e27-e55b-73d1-87d8-4e01f1f75043\\r\\n019c7714-3b77-74d1-9866-e1f484aae2ab\\r\\n\\u003c/rollout_ids\\u003e\\r\\n\\u003c/oai-mem-citation\\u003e\\r\\n```\\r\\n- `citation_entries` is for rendering:\\r\\n  - one citation entry per line\\r\\n  - format: `\\u003cfile\\u003e:\\u003cline_start\\u003e-\\u003cline_end\\u003e|note=[\\u003chow memory was used\\u003e]`\\r\\n  - use file paths relative to the memory base path (for example, `MEMORY.md`,\\r\\n    `rollout_summaries/...`, `skills/...`)\\r\\n  - only cite files actually used under the memory base path (do not cite\\r\\n    workspace files as memory citations)\\r\\n  - if you used `MEMORY.md` and then a rollout summary/skill file, cite both\\r\\n  - list entries in order of importance (most important first)\\r\\n  - `note` should be short, single-line, and use simple characters only (avoid\\r\\n    unusual symbols, no newlines)\\r\\n- `rollout_ids` is for us to track what previous rollouts you find useful:\\r\\n  - include one rollout id per line\\r\\n  - rollout ids should look like UUIDs (for example,\\r\\n    `019c6e27-e55b-73d1-87d8-4e01f1f75043`)\\r\\n  - include unique ids only; do not repeat ids\\r\\n  - an empty `\\u003crollout_ids\\u003e` section is allowed if no rollout ids are available\\r\\n  - you can find rollout ids in rollout summary files and MEMORY.md\\r\\n  - do not include file paths or notes in this section\\r\\n  - For every `citation_entries`, try to find and cite the corresponding rollout id if possible\\r\\n- Never include memory citations inside pull-request messages.\\r\\n- Never cite blank lines; double-check ranges.\\r\\n\\r\\nUpdating memories:\\r\\n\\r\\nYou can update the memories **only** when explicitly asked by the user. This must always come from a direct request from the user.\\r\\n- Write your update in C:\\\\Users\\\\kkids\\\\.codex\\\\memories/extensions/ad_hoc/notes/\\r\\n- Each update must be one small file containing what you want to add/delete/update from the memories.\\r\\n- The name of this file must be `\\u003ctimestamp\\u003e-\\u003cshort slug\\u003e.md`\\r\\n- Do not try to edit the memory files yourself, only add one update note in C:\\\\Users\\\\kkids\\\\.codex\\\\memories/extensions/ad_hoc/notes/\\r\\n\\r\\n========= MEMORY_SUMMARY BEGINS =========\\r\\nv1\\n\\n## User Profile\\n\\nThe user does evidence-heavy Windows work through Codex across AERO/BF6 tuning, cautious game/launcher repair, portable diagnostics, and the `Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT` Next.js/Prisma app. They value truth boundaries: read-only phases stay read-only, dry-runs must be honest, and admin-only steps stop at an explicit handoff. They prefer practical artifacts over long chat dumps: runnable commands, reports, rollback scripts, validation files, portable bundles, and exact Git handoffs. For code changes, they favor extending the existing architecture with tests and real persisted/audited behavior rather than client-only appearances. For repair/tuning, they expect reversible steps, current-state verification, and a clear distinction between prepared, applied, and live-verified state.\\n\\n## User preferences\\n\\n- Before implementing in a missing or cross-device checkout, discover/synchronize the authoritative repository, review required handoff/conversation material, preserve local changes, and report branch, commit, remote parity, validation, and remaining work.\\n- For repository setup, inspect before modification; use the existing launcher/architecture, preserve `.env`, databases, and local work, and make a diagnostic backup before an explicit state rebuild.\\n- When an explicitly identified newest branch is authoritative, keep it for overlapping files and retain older work only when additive; verify remote branches/ancestry and fast-forward `main` before cleanup.\\n- For stateful app commands, verify persistence, audit/event creation, and live user effects; do not treat UI feedback alone as success.\\n- Default early analysis or baseline phases to strict no-touch behavior until the user explicitly moves into a change phase.\\n- Preserve epistemic status: mark missing evidence as a gap instead of guessing.\\n- For repair/recovery, ask before uninstalling or deleting major app/game folders; prefer process checks, logs, and reversible rename/quarantine first.\\n- When the user asks for a report or artifact, create the file artifact, not only chat text.\\n- For cross-PC Windows handoffs, save the bundle in â€œan obvious spotâ€ and tell the next agent to discover paths/hardware instead of hard-coding the original machine.\\n- For admin-blocked work, do not claim success: build/validate the worker, give the exact Administrator command, and name the status/log files to reopen.\\n- In practical automation phases, produce executable scripts plus validation rather than documentation-only scaffolding.\\n- In AeroTune, default sensors to 3-second polling; when safe, prefer app-owned PresentMon automation to manual CSV recording.\\n- For a simple â€œhow do I start the app?â€ request, lead with the exact runnable command.\\n\\n## General Tips\\n\\n- In shared-database browser tests, run mutations in Chromium only; retain WebKit for read-only/accessibility/responsive coverage. Copy Unicode locators exactly to avoid mojibake timeouts.\\n- Windows PowerShell 5.1 is a recurring constraint; parser-check the final script in the real runtime and avoid automatic-variable collisions such as $Pid/$PID.\\n- For portable collectors, keep the read-only boundary, bound sampling, redact shareable output, and create numbered sibling output folders rather than deleting prior diagnostics.\\n- For AERO elevation boundaries, use `skills/windows-admin-handoff/SKILL.md`: validate locally, provide exact admin/rollback commands, then wait for user-run evidence.\\n- For BF6 performance questions, inspect BF6 config and NVIDIA Profile Inspector exports together; in-game refresh alone can mislead.\\n- For local power-plan recovery, `powercfg /list` is the live truth source; an export failure such as `0x522` does not block switch-and-verify.\\n- For current AeroTune work, `Start-AeroTune.ps1 -DryRun -Json` is the fast truth source for the runnable phase.\\n\\n## What\\u0027s in Memory\\n\\n### C:\\\\Users\\\\kkids\\\\Documents\\\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\n\\n#### 2026-07-18\\n\\n- Tall Tale Platform and local Windows validation: Tall Tale Platform, startTaleSession, PlaythroughMembership, Invitation, shortCodeHash, npm run dev:full, db:preset, npm run validate\\n  - desc: Search first for current `Codex_TreasureHunt` startup, canonical-main integration, Player/Captain/Creator platform behavior, invitation handling, or release validation.\\n  - learnings: `dev:full` preserves normal state; preset only after backing up stale SQLite. I’ll widen only the temporary audit assertion, restore the isolated campaign copy to sequence 0 from its preserved pre-run backup, and run once more to observe the remaining replay, refresh, section, offline/reconnect, and viewport checks.

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-18T17:07:09.874Z

I found and am correcting an isolation hazard before proceeding: despite the detached source worktree, Next’s inferred root caused the port-3100 server to use the main checkout’s Prisma database. The last audit run added exactly two progression events (`CHAPTER_PREPARED` and `CHAPTER_RELEASED`) plus audit login rows to the main database. I am stopping the audit runtime now, backing up the current main database into the ignored audit area, and will transactionally remove only rows created by this audit and restore only the affected chapter/campaign fields after verifying them against the pre-run isolated snapshot. I will not replace the database wholesale or disturb unrelated rows.

  workdir: "C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt",
  timeout_ms: 30000
});

**Timestamp:** 2026-07-18T17:09:17.835Z

The database repair is complete and verified: the main campaign is back at sequence 0 with chapter 1 locked, audit-created progression/snapshot/view rows are gone, login attempts are back to the pre-turn count of 14, and port 3000 stayed continuously owned by PID 30516. Port 3100 is stopped, and the detached baseline has no tracked modifications. The failed release run remains valid evidence: seal/parchment/ink stages appeared and replay became available, but completion took 10,081 ms and failed the baseline’s own upper bound. No further runtime retry is safe until the application supports an explicit database path independent of Next’s inferred root, so the remaining work is documentation and validation.

const r = await tools.shell_command({
  command: "@'\nfrom pathlib import Path\nimport re, collections\nc=collections.Counter(); loc=collections.defaultdict(list)\nfor p in Path('src').rglob('*'):\n if p.suffix not in {'.tsx','.ts'} or '.test.' in p.name: continue\n s=p.read_text(encoding='utf-8')\n for i,line in enumerate(s.splitlines(),1):\n  for m in re.finditer(r'data-scene-part=\"([A-Za-z0-9-]+)\"',line): c[m.group(1)]+=1;loc[m.group(1)].append(f'{p.as_posix()}:{i}')\nfor k,v in c.most_common(): print(f'{v:2} {k:28} {\";\".join(loc[k])}')\nprint('TOTAL_LITERAL_PART_ATTRIBUTES',sum(c.values()),'UNIQUE',len(c))\n'@ | python -; '=== ASSET CONTRACTS ==='; Get-Content -Raw src\\animation\\assets\\rive-contracts.ts; Get-Content -Raw src\\animation\\assets\\lottie-contracts.ts; Get-Content -Raw src\\animation\\core\\asset-registry.ts",
  workdir: "C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\artifacts\\validation\\animation-audit\\baseline",
  timeout_ms: 30000
});
