Executive summary\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:7:The baseline has a substantial animation framework, but it does not yet have animation truth. The audit inventories **220** current effects, runtime contracts, and missing opportunities. Of the 28 registered GSAP scenes, **20** have a production call path and **8** are development-showcase-only, dead, or redundant. All 28 can build and finish without a required visible target. Therefore a completed timeline, a green builder test, and a clean showcase run do not prove that a user saw the intended object.\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:9:The most serious chain is in Player progression: root-scoped scenes query whichever section happens to be mounted; generic part names can match permanent content, a temporary event overlay, PageFlip sources, or PageFlip clones; presentation errors are caught; the snapshot refresh and viewed acknowledgment still continue. A mandatory event can consequently be marked viewed even when no required content was visible. Chapter release also keeps replay only in `lastRelease` component state, so refresh loses it. The live baseline run did render seal, parchment, and ink stages and exposed Replay Ceremony, but measured **10,081 ms**, failing the existing `<10,000 ms` acceptance limit.\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:11:All four named production Rive contracts lack a `.riv` path: mounted Invitation Seal and Finale Mechanism render static fallbacks, Journal Clasp and Voyage Compass are unmounted, and the only `.riv` is a development sample. Modern sign-in, Invitation Ceremony, Library, waiting-room, and Studio flows largely bypass the legacy cinematic paths and lack a unified Motion policy. Reduced motion has three competing authorities: custom product/system resolution, `MotionConfig reducedMotion=\"never\"`, and CSS media queries.\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:13:### Controlling counts\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:15:| Category                   | Count | Meaning in this audit Audit baseline and methodology\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:31:- Authoritative application snapshot: `4dbe8c0ae2fbab2785d1d3f26b8d7ba33bf56aee` in a detached worktree under `artifacts/validation/animation-audit/baseline`. Its tracked source is clean at that commit; two untracked audit database backups remain (`prisma/dev.db.pre-clean-20260718-125935` and `prisma/dev.db.rate-limit-polluted-20260718-125728`).\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:32:- The main checkout advanced only through synchronization commits during the audit and contained substantial concurrent uncommitted work. That work was treated as out of baseline and left untouched.\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:33:- Static work covered registry definitions, callers, triggers, selectors, `data-scene-part`, `data-gsap-owned`, `data-motion-owned`, `data-journal-actor`, `data-journal-phase`, state machines, library wrappers, assets, CSS, audio, event mapping, replay and acknowledgment paths.\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:34:- Dynamic work used an isolated port 3100 only after verifying port ownership; port 3000 was never reused or stopped. Focused tests and live target measurements were retained from the original audit task.\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:35:- Truth rule: **build/run/complete is not visible success**. Runtime proof requires an expected trigger, correct visible instance, required target count, uniqueness, stable final semantic state, and correct persistence/acknowledgment behavior.\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:36:- One final mutation flow was stopped when Next.js root inference proved the detached server was using the main checkout database. Only exact rows created by the audit run were transactionally removed after a backup and safety assertions. No further mutation run was attempted without a provably isolated database path.\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:38:### Relevant changes newer than the audit baseline\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:40:The following main-checkout work existed concurrently and may improve some surfaces after `4dbe8c0ae2fbab2785d1d3f26b8d7ba33bf56aee`. access-gates.spec.ts; tall-tale-platform.spec.ts                                   | Preserved; baseline runtime used the detached commit, not these edits                |\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:50:## 3. Current architecture\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:52:`AnimationProvider` exposes product mode, `AnimationDirector`, audio, metrics, and asset/runtime state. The director resolves one of 28 registry scenes and gives the builder a root-scoped selector helper. Scene builders create GSAP timelines and semantic labels; Player and Quartermaster call them from event/action handlers. Motion independently owns React presence/layout. StPageFlip clones hidden React page sources into a separate visible host. Rive and Lottie wrappers mount asset contracts. CSS data attributes and a Web Animations observer drive the journal-opening phase chain.\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:54:The layering choices are sound in principle: GSAP for narrative sequencing, Motion for UI presence/layout, StPageFlip for physical curl, Rive for stateful authored objects, Lottie for decorative loops/one-shots, CSS for materials/ambient states, WAAPI for observing CSS completion, and Web Audio for semantic cues. The defect is coordination: target identity, ownership, reduced motion, interruption, final state, replay, and event acknowledgment are not governed by one enforceable contract.\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:56:### Modern platform versus legacy cinematic paths\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:58:| Surface                  | Modern platform path at baseline                                                                 | Legacy cinematic path at baseline                                                | Verified consequence                                                           |\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:59:| ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:60:| Player sign-in           | PlayerSignIn posts directly and routes; no AnimationDirector call                                | AccessGate calls `player-access` around the access operation                     | Modern authentication bypasses the registered access scene                     |\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:61:| Captain/Creator sign-in  | StaffSignIn handles both roles with direct fetch/router; no AnimationDirector call               | Quartermaster calls `quartermaster-login`                                        | Modern role sign-ins bypass the chart-room login scene                         |\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:62:| Invitation ceremony      | InvitationCeremony is a direct lookup/PIN/accept form and router flow; no Rive mount             | AccessGate and Quartermaster mount Invitation Seal, but only as fallback         | The named modern ceremony is not a real Rive ceremony                          |\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:63:| Libraries/waiting/studio | Modern mutation, polling, filtering and editor flows are mostly static; Studio drag uses dnd-kit | PlayerExperience and Quartermaster contain the dense GSAP/Motion cinematic layer | The product has two motion dialects and no shared platform transition contract |\r\nC:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt\\Development_Docs\\Animation_System_Full_Audit.md:65:## 4.
